---
title: "Roadmap"
---

How every module depends on the others. Blue nodes are reusable
[foundations](../../foundations/README.md) modules shared across courses; the green node
is a prerequisite *course*; arrows mean "needed for".

Like every course, this one assumes the notation grammar in
[reading-math-notation](../../foundations/reading-math-notation/lesson.qmd) — take it
first if the symbols aren't yet second nature.

This course also **builds on** [Approximate Bayes Factors](../approximate-bayes-factors/syllabus.md)
(it starts from the per-SNP Wakefield log Bayes factors `wakefield_abf(beta, se, W)` you
built there) and reuses its anchors rather than re-teaching them; if the ABF is rusty,
skim its syllabus first.

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    classDef foundation fill:#e8f0fe,stroke:#4285f4
    classDef priorcourse fill:#e6f4ea,stroke:#34a853

    F1["distributions-and-densities<br/>(dnorm, N(mu, sigma^2))"]:::foundation
    F2["likelihood-and-log-likelihood<br/>(work in log space)"]:::foundation
    F3["priors-posteriors-and-updating<br/>(prior x evidence)"]:::foundation
    F4["marginal-likelihood-and-model-evidence<br/>(average over which SNP)"]:::foundation
    F5["bayes-factors-and-posterior-odds<br/>(evidence x prior, normalise)"]:::foundation

    C1["course: approximate-bayes-factors<br/>(per-SNP lABF: l1, l2)"]:::priorcourse

    M1["01 · The colocalisation question<br/>(two studies, one region; l1, l2)"]
    M2["02 · Five hypotheses<br/>(H0..H4; PP3 distinct vs PP4 shared; p1,p2,p12)"]
    M3["03 · Combining ABFs with priors<br/>(lH0..lH4; the i!=j trick; coloc_abf)"]
    M4["04 · Capstone<br/>(decode coloc.abf; shared vs distinct)"]

    F2 --> F4
    F3 --> F4
    F1 --> F4
    F4 --> F5
    F3 --> F5

    C1 --> M1
    F1 --> M1
    M1 --> M2
    F3 --> M2
    M2 --> M3
    F2 --> M3
    F4 --> M3
    F5 --> M3
    M3 --> M4
```

## Suggested order

1. Foundation: [distributions-and-densities](../../foundations/distributions-and-densities/lesson.qmd) —
   new? take it fully; done before? skim its pronunciation table.
2. Foundation: [likelihood-and-log-likelihood](../../foundations/likelihood-and-log-likelihood/lesson.qmd) —
   same rule.
3. Foundation: [priors-posteriors-and-updating](../../foundations/priors-posteriors-and-updating/lesson.qmd) —
   same rule.
4. Foundation: [marginal-likelihood-and-model-evidence](../../foundations/marginal-likelihood-and-model-evidence/lesson.qmd) —
   the engine behind each hypothesis's support.
5. Foundation: [bayes-factors-and-posterior-odds](../../foundations/bayes-factors-and-posterior-odds/lesson.qmd) —
   take it after marginal-likelihood.
6. [01 · The colocalisation question](modules/01-the-colocalisation-question/lesson.qmd)
   (+ [practice](modules/01-the-colocalisation-question/practice.qmd))
7. [02 · Five hypotheses](modules/02-five-hypotheses/lesson.qmd)
   (+ [practice](modules/02-five-hypotheses/practice.qmd))
8. [03 · Combining ABFs with priors](modules/03-combining-abfs-with-priors/lesson.qmd)
   (+ [practice](modules/03-combining-abfs-with-priors/practice.qmd))
9. [04 · Capstone — reading coloc.abf](modules/04-capstone-reading-coloc-abf/lesson.qmd)
   (+ [practice](modules/04-capstone-reading-coloc-abf/practice.qmd))

After each lesson, do its practice page before moving on — the problems are the part that
makes it stick. External videos/posts for each module: [resources](resources.md).
